steps.title: Put the equation in standard form
steps.content:
Expand both sides and move all terms to one side.
$$x(x+2)-40=4x(x-6)$$
$$x^2+2x-40=4x^2-24x$$
$$-3x^2+26x-40=0$$

steps.title: Use the sum shortcut
steps.content:
For $ax^2+bx+c=0$, the sum of the solutions is $-\frac{b}{a}$. Here, $a=-3$ and $b=26$.
$$-\frac{b}{a}=-\frac{26}{-3}=\frac{26}{3}$$
The answer is 26/3.
